[[Sam Burgess]] (brother) }} }} '''Luke Burgess''' (born 20 February 1987) is an English former professional [[rugby league]] footballer who last played for the [[Salford Red Devils]] in the [[Super League]]. Luke Burgess is the brother of fellow rugby league players [[Sam Burgess|Sam]], [[George Burgess (rugby league)|George]] and [[Tom Burgess (rugby league)|Tom Burgess]]. He previously played in the NRL for the [[South Sydney Rabbitohs]] and the [[Manly-Warringah Sea Eagles]]. ==Background== Burgess was born on 20 February 1987 in [[Liversedge]], [[Kirklees]], [[West Yorkshire]], [[England]]. His late father, Mark Burgess who died of [[Motor neurone disease]], was also a [[rugby league]] footballer who played for [[Mansfield Marksman|Nottingham City]], [[Rochdale Hornets]], [[Dewsbury Rams|Dewsbury]] and [[Hunslet R.L.F.C.|Hunslet]];{{cite news|last=Irvine|first=Christopher|newspaper=The Times Online|url=http://www.timesonline.co.uk/tol/sport/rugby_league/article2741406.ece|title=Sam Burgess inspired by memory of late father|date=26 October 2007|access-date=17 September 2009}}{{dead link|date=September 2024|bot=medic}}{{cbignore|bot=medic}} whilst his mother Julie is a teacher, currently employed at [[The Scots College]] in [[Bellevue Hill, New South Wales|Bellevue Hill]], Sydney.{{Cite web |url=http://www.dailytelegraph.com.au/entertainment/sydney-confidential/meet-the-magnificent-mrs-burgess/story-fni0cvc9-1226690449682 |title=Meet the magnificent MRS Burgess | dailytelegraph.com.au |access-date=3 October 2013 |archive-date=11 April 2016 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20160411170632/http://www.dailytelegraph.com.au/entertainment/sydney-confidential/meet-the-magnificent-mrs-burgess/story-fni0cvc9-1226690449682 |url-status=dead }} His younger brothers [[Sam Burgess|Sam]], [[Tom Burgess (rugby league)|Tom]] and [[George Burgess (rugby league)|George]] play professional rugby league with the [[South Sydney Rabbitohs]], while Sam previously played rugby union for [[Bath Rugby]]. He attended [[Heckmondwike Grammar School]] to receive a secondary education before becoming a professional rugby league footballer. He played his junior rugby for Dewsbury Moor, and also played for Liversedge Cricket Club. ==Professional playing career== [[File:Luke Burgess with Super League cup.jpg|thumb|left|Luke Burgess with the Super League trophy in 2009.]] ===Super League=== Burgess played {{rlp|pr}} for the [[Leeds Rhinos]] having previously been on loan to [[London Broncos#2006–2011: Harlequins Rugby League|Harlequins RL]] and [[Doncaster R.L.F.C.|Doncaster]]. Burgess' début game was against [[Castleford Tigers]] at [[The Jungle (Wheldon Road)|Wheldon Road]], Leeds lost this game. Burgess scored his first try on his second game against [[London Broncos#2006–2011: Harlequins Rugby League|Harlequins RL]]. At the end of the 2008 season, Burgess became the inaugural winner of the Albert Goldthorpe Rookie of the Year Award, presented by the leading trade newspaper, ''[[Rugby Leaguer & League Express]]''.{{cite web|title=The Albert Goldthorpe Awards|url=http://www.totalrl.com/web/the-albert-goldthorpe-awards-roll-of-honour|work=totalrl.com|publisher=League Publications|access-date=16 February 2014|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20140326074057/http://www.totalrl.com/web/the-albert-goldthorpe-awards-roll-of-honour/|archive-date=26 March 2014|url-status=dead}} In 2009, Burgess played in 28 games, however, he only scored one try for the club against [[Salford Red Devils|Salford City Reds]], compared to five tries from only five appearances in 2008. He played in the [[2009 Super League Grand Final]] victory over [[St Helens R.F.C.|St. Helens]] at [[Old Trafford]].{{cite news|last=Fletcher|first=Paul|title=St Helens 10-18 Leeds Rhinos|url=https://news.bbc.co.uk/sport2/hi/rugby_league/8290503.stm|access-date=22 November 2019|newspaper=BBC Sport|date=10 October 2009}} On 21 June 2011 it was confirmed that Luke had signed for the [[South Sydney Rabbitohs]], joining brothers [[Sam Burgess|Sam]] and [[George Burgess (rugby league)|George]] at the club who play in the Australian [[NRL]]. Brother Tom Burgess came later.{{cite news|url=http://www.skysports.com/story/0,19528,12211_6998665,00.html|title=Family affair at Rabbitohs|date=21 June 2011|work=Sky Sports}} ===National Rugby League=== During the [[2011 NRL season]] Burgess was named to make his NRL début for Souths having been in Australia for less than two weeks.{{cite news|last=Nicolussi|first=Christian|title=Luke Burgess named to make NRL début with South Sydney|url=http://www.couriermail.com.au/sport/nrl/luke-burgess-named-to-make-nrl-debut-with-south-sydney/story-e6frep5x-1226083839709|access-date=19 October 2013|newspaper=The Daily Telegraph|date=29 June 2011}} Luke was included in England's 2012 training squad but ultimately never played an international game. https://www.skysports.com/rugby-league/news/12204/11510830/luke-burgess-retires-after-helping-salford-secure-super-league-status In May 2013, model Yolanda Hodgson who had been a contestant on ''[[Australia's Next Top Model (cycle 7)|Australia's Next Top Model]]'' in 2011, gave birth to the couple's first child, daughter Grace Luca.{{cite news|url=http://www.news.com.au/national-news/nsw-act/nrls-luke-burgess-and-model-yolanda-hodgson-welcome-baby-grace-to-the-world/story-fndo4bst-1226637812696|work=The Daily Telegraph|title=NRL's Luke Burgess and model Yolanda Hodgson welcome baby Grace to the world|date=8 May 2013}} In August 2013, the Burgess brothers became the first set of four brothers to line up in the same Australian side since [[Ray Norman|Ray]], [[Roy Norman|Roy]], [[Rex Norman|Rex]] and [[Bernard Norman]] played for Sydney's [[Annandale (rugby league team)|Annandale club]] in the [[1910 NSWRFL season]].{{cite news|title=Rugby league history as FOUR English brothers star for Russell Crowe's side|url=https://www.mirror.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/burgess-brothers-make-rugby-league-2239417|access-date=19 October 2013|newspaper=[[The Daily Mirror]]|date=30 August 2013}} However Luke missed out on the clubs 2014 premiership after he was named on the extended bench for their grand final victory over the Bulldogs.https://www.theroar.com.au/2014/09/30/2014-nrl-grand-final-full-team-lists/ In 2015, he joined the Manly Warringah Sea Eagles, after being granted an early leave by the [[South Sydney Rabbitohs]], after an incident at a club after the Rabbitohs' high-altitude training camp in [[Arizona|Arizona, United States]]. It was alleged that a doorman working at an Arizona bar had told Burgess and South Sydney captain [[John Sutton (rugby league)|John Sutton]] to leave as they were intoxicated. Sutton allegedly became restless and Burgess got into a fight with the bouncer. Images were released of the pair handcuffed. The bouncer eventually dropped all charges after reaching an agreement with the two players.{{cite web|url=https://www.foxsports.com.au/nrl/nrl-premiership/the-66-scandals-in-four-years-that-have-rocked-the-nrl/news-story/1bbcdffd432063bec8e521c736f7ae4d/|title=The 66 scandals in four years that have rocked the NRL|website=Fox Sports|date=8 February 2019}} ==References== {{Reflist}} ==External links== {{commons category|Luke Burgess (rugby league)}} *[https://www.salfordreddevils.net/team/luke-burgess/ Salford Red Devils profile] *[https://web.archive.org/web/20070927215946/http://leedsrugby.dnsupdate.co.uk/leedsrhinos/Squad/squad-page.asp?ID=168 Leeds Rhinos profile] *[https://web.archive.org/web/20110108080513/http://superleague.co.uk/profile.php?id=2424 Super League profile] *[https://web.archive.org/web/20070928015527/http://www.quins.co.uk/newspage.ink?nid=37279&newstype=n&matchid=&storytype=RL Luke on Loan] *[https://web.archive.org/web/20070928015627/http://www.quins.co.uk/newspage.ink?nid=37293&newstype=n&matchid=&Refid=&storytype=RL Mac Full of Praise] *[http://www.rugby-league.com/superleague/player-info/2424 SL profile] {{Leeds Rhinos - 2009 Super League Grand Final winners}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Burgess, Luke}} [[Category:1987 births]] [[Category:Living people]] [[Category:Burgess family|Luke]] [[Category:Catalans Dragons players]] [[Category:Doncaster R.L.F.C. players]] [[Category:English rugby league players]] [[Category:Leeds Rhinos players]] [[Category:London Broncos players]] [[Category:Manly Warringah Sea Eagles players]] [[Category:People educated at Heckmondwike Grammar School]] [[Category:People from Liversedge]] [[Category:Sportspeople from Kirklees]] [[Category:Rugby league players from West Yorkshire]] [[Category:Rugby league players from Wakefield]] [[Category:Rugby league props]] [[Category:Salford RLFC players]] [[Category:South Sydney Rabbitohs players]]
